item.title: Th9 cells at the intersection of IBD and colorectal cancer: a context-dependent factor in carcinogenesis.
item.description: Inflammation and regeneration - Review - The differentiation of naïve T cells into the Th9 phenotype is driven by the synergistic effects of IL-4 and TGF-β, leading to the secretion of their hallmark cytokine, interleukin-9 (IL-9). Known ...

item.title: Evaluation of Obesity as an Independent Risk Factor for Colorectal Dysplasia Development in Inflammatory Bowel Disease: A Matched Case-Control Study.
item.description: Digestive diseases and sciences - Chronic intestinal inflammation is a well-established driver of colorectal dysplasia in inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). However, the role of metabolic factors such as obesity remains poorly under...

item.title: LL-37: Biological Mechanisms and Emerging Therapeutic Applications in Intestinal Disease.
item.description: Immunity, inflammation and disease - Review - Human cathelicidin peptide LL-37 is encoded by the CAMP gene and plays a key role in innate immunity. It maintains intestinal homeostasis through antibacterial, immunomodulation, and tissue repair ...

item.title: Additional Crypt Phenotypes Lined With High-Grade Cellular Dysplasia in Protruding Growths in Ulcerative Colitis.
item.description: APMIS : acta pathologica, microbiologica, et immunologica Scandinavica - Two crypt phenotypes (ACP): crypt rings in tandem (CRT) and crypts with lateral budding's (CLB's) were recently found interpolated amidst generally accepted crypt phenotypes (GACP, i.e., tubular, t...

item.title: Surgical management of ulcerative colitis-associated colorectal cancer in a 20-year period, a single-centre study.
item.description: Pathology oncology research : POR - The incidence of inflammatory bowel disease is on the rise. Inflammation that persists for years or decades may involve the risk of malignant transformation. Indeed, it is the cause of death in 15%...
